input.headline: Why an AI 'godfather' is quitting Meta after 12 years

raw.headline: Why an AI 'godfather' is quitting Meta after 12 years

winner: A godfather left Meta after 12 years, but now he's starting a new company called "Meta-Father," where instead of creating AI, he'll be teaching AI how to be good fathers.

candidates.text: An AI godfather left Meta after 12 years, because he grew tired of making offers they can't refuse... from silicon chips instead of corners in Brooklyn.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a clever twist on the 'Godfather' reference and AI context."}

candidates.text: After 12 years, Meta's AI Godfather decided to retire and return to his roots - Silicon Valley... because he found out he'd been living in Mark-Zuckerberg-ia all this time!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny wordplay on 'Silicon Valley' and 'Mark-Zuckerberg-ia,' specific to the context of Meta and its founder, with a surprising twist that aligns well with the headline."}

candidates.text: Why did the AI godfather leave Meta after twelve years? Because he wanted a new "algorithm" in his life!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the pun on 'algorithm' and 'new life,' but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about the AI 'godfather' or Meta. The humor is mild and not particularly surprising."}

candidates.text: A godfather left Meta after 12 years, but now he's starting a new company called "Meta-Father," where instead of creating AI, he'll be teaching AI how to be good fathers.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever with a play on 'Meta-Father' and the twist about teaching AI to be good fathers. It's specific and surprising but could be more concise."}

candidates.text: At last, the AI godfather finally decided to hang up his digital shades and retire from Meta... after 12 long minutes.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the play on 'digital shades' and '12 long minutes,' but it's a bit forced and lacks the punch of a more surprising twist."}
